picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: Yumna Al-Arashi — Body as Resistance
picks.blurb: First solo museum show for Al-Arashi — photography, sculpture and book confront the policing of women’s bodies.

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan

picks.title: American Identities
picks.blurb: Levinthal’s staged photos and ChicanX prints pick apart the myths and power plays of American identity.

picks.title: Eye(s) Open
picks.blurb: Eleven artists confront Eye’s 2,000 colonial-era films from Indonesia and Suriname with new works.
picks.why: Indoor · ferry trip · the striking Eye building

picks.title: Danh Vo at the Stedelijk
picks.blurb: Danh Vo weaves objects, images and text into a quiet meditation on identity and human ties.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Moeders — Dutch home cooking
picks.blurb: Walls papered with strangers’ mothers’ photos and a stamppot-and-stew menu built for a grey evening.
picks.why: Snug · classic Dutch · ideal in the rain

picks.title: Café de Klos — ribs & grill
picks.blurb: A tiny, packed grill house off the canals famous for sticky spare ribs — no frills, no reservations.
picks.why: Warm · cheap · duck in out of the wet

picks.title: Wynand Fockink — jenever tasting
picks.blurb: A 1679 tasting room behind Dam Square pouring its own jenevers and liqueurs — you bend to the brimful glass.
picks.why: Historic · indoor · made for a downpour

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Eye Filmmuseum — repertory screening
picks.blurb: Across the IJ by free ferry: a striking film museum with a daily repertory programme and a river-view café.
picks.why: Indoor · a film + the ferry ride · weather-proof

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Tony’s Chocolonely Super Store
picks.blurb: The flagship of the slavery-free chocolate brand — make-your-own bars, a café, and a serious sweet-tooth detour.
picks.why: Indoor · kid-friendly · a happy rainy hour

picks.title: ARTIS Royal Zoo
picks.blurb: The country’s oldest zoo — big cats, an aquarium and a planetarium across a leafy 19th-century park.
picks.why: All-day with kids · indoor + outdoor mix

picks.title: NEMO Science Museum
picks.blurb: Five floors of hands-on experiments in the big green ship — plus a free rooftop with city views.

picks.title: Micropia
picks.blurb: The world’s only microbe museum — microscopes, a kiss-o-meter and the tiny life all around us.

picks.title: Het Scheepvaartmuseum
picks.blurb: Climb aboard a full-size replica of an 18th-century VOC ship, then explore maps, globes and sea tales.
picks.why: Indoor · the ship is a winner

picks.title: Vondelpark playgrounds
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s favourite park: open-air playgrounds, the Kinderkookkafé, ducks and room to run.
picks.why: Free · easy any time with kids

picks.title: Goat Farm Ridammerhoeve
picks.blurb: A working organic goat farm in the forest — bottle-feed the kids (the goat kind), then pancakes on the terrace.

picks.title: TunFun indoor playground
picks.blurb: A giant indoor adventure playground hidden in a former traffic underpass — slides, ball pits, trampolines.

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.

picks.title: Heritage Tram rides
picks.blurb: Ride a beautifully restored vintage tram from Haarlemmermeerstation out through the Amsterdamse Bos.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Brut de Mer
picks.blurb: Oysters, fish and bubbles — a tiny, lively seafood bar in De Pijp.

picks.title: Buffet van Odette
picks.blurb: A bright canal-side all-day spot for honest, seasonal cooking and very good cakes.

picks.title: Café Caron
picks.blurb: A warm neighbourhood bistro from the Caron brothers — classic French plates, done properly.

picks.title: Bar Centraal
picks.blurb: A buzzy natural-wine bar with small sharing plates on a corner in Oud-West.

picks.title: Choux
picks.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

picks.title: The Cottage
picks.blurb: Comfort food and good drinks at a snug De Pijp corner with street-side benches.

picks.title: Entrepot
picks.blurb: Seasonal sharing plates and natural wine in a light industrial hall in Amsterdam Oost.

picks.title: FC Hyena
picks.blurb: An arthouse cinema with a wine bar and a small kitchen, right on the IJ in Noord.

picks.title: Bar Fisk
picks.blurb: Sun-soaked Mediterranean seafood and sharing plates in De Pijp.

picks.title: Gebr. Hartering
picks.blurb: No menu — just whatever’s good that day. Adventurous, generous chef’s cooking by the old harbour.

picks.title: Le Hollandais
picks.blurb: Honest French-Dutch cooking — game, offal, oysters — a long-running Amsteldijk favourite.

picks.title: Kaagman & Kortekaas
picks.blurb: A tiny open-kitchen bistro doing refined, produce-led cooking in the old centre.

picks.title: Café de Klepel
picks.blurb: A candlelit wine bistro with a daily-changing French menu and a deep, characterful list.
picks.why: Daily French menu · deep wine list

picks.title: Café Modern
picks.blurb: A set seasonal menu in a characterful former bank across the IJ in Amsterdam Noord.

picks.title: Bar Parry
picks.blurb: A neighbourhood natural-wine bar with a short, sharp plate menu in De Pijp.

picks.title: Rijsel
picks.blurb: Lille-inspired French cooking in a converted school — that rotisserie chicken is the order.

picks.title: Scheepskameel
picks.blurb: Open-kitchen cooking and a riesling-deep list in a bright hall on Kattenburg.

picks.title: Toscanini
picks.blurb: A beloved Jordaan institution for fresh, regional Italian cooking; reserve well in advance.

picks.title: De Vrouw met de Baard
picks.blurb: An easy all-day neighbourhood spot in Oost — coffee, wine and seasonal plates.

picks.title: Wilde Zwijnen
picks.blurb: Seasonal, ingredient-driven Dutch cooking (the name means “wild boar”) in the Indische Buurt.

picks.title: Petit Gâteau
picks.blurb: Jewel-like little tarts — pistachio, lemon, chocolate — from the patisserie counter at the Foodhallen.

picks.title: The Frozen Fountain
picks.blurb: The country’s best-known design store — floors of furniture, lighting and covetable objects to browse.

picks.title: Brewery Homeland
picks.blurb: A craft brewery and beach bar on the IJ — tank-fresh beer and a waterfront sun-trap terrace.

picks.title: Libertine Petit Café
picks.blurb: A funky canal-side bar-bistro at the Noordermarkt — French classics, oysters and a stellar brunch.

picks.title: Noorderlicht
picks.blurb: A greenhouse café on the NDSM wharf — string lights, salvaged charm and a big waterside terrace.

picks.title: Café de Tuin
picks.blurb: A quintessential Jordaan brown café — newspapers, good beer and an easy neighbourhood buzz.

picks.title: The Duchess
picks.blurb: An opulent belle-époque brasserie in a former bank — marble, brass and a long oyster-and-champagne bar.

picks.title: Mata Hari
picks.blurb: A warm canal-side bar-restaurant on the Wallen — candlelit, characterful and a local antidote to the crowds.

picks.title: De Ceuvel
picks.blurb: A café on a reclaimed shipyard — a circular-economy experiment with a ramshackle, plant-filled terrace.

picks.title: Skatecafé
picks.blurb: A working skate ramp inside a former garage, with a kitchen, bar and DJs — as fun as it sounds.
picks.why: A skate ramp in a restaurant

picks.title: Patisserie Holtkamp
picks.blurb: A century-old patisserie behind a gorgeous Amsterdam School facade — famous for its kroketten and gateaux.

picks.title: De Filmhallen
picks.blurb: A nine-screen arthouse cinema in a converted tram depot — with a bustling food hall right next door.
picks.why: Arthouse · in the old tram depot

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Sexyland World
picks.blurb: A members’ club owned by 1,000 people — a different host and a different vibe every single night.

picks.title: Garage Noord
picks.blurb: A club, kitchen and cultural space in a former garage — daytime plates, late-night dancing.
picks.why: Kitchen by day · club by night

picks.title: Conservatorium Hotel
picks.blurb: The glass-roofed lounge of the city’s grandest design hotel — a stunning spot for a drink or high tea.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Athenaeum Boekhandel
picks.blurb: The intellectual heart of the Spui — a beloved bookshop and an international newsstand packed floor-to-ceiling.

picks.title: The American Book Center
picks.blurb: A sprawling three-floor English-language bookshop — a spiral of shelves and a basement of zines and comics.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Friday Next
picks.blurb: A concept store and café in one — young Dutch design to browse, with brunch and coffee while you do it.

picks.title: WonderWood
picks.blurb: A gallery-shop devoted to plywood and mid-century modern — bentwood chairs and design classics since 1999.

picks.title: RIJKS®
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk’s Michelin-starred dining room beside the Rijksmuseum — the “cuisine of the Low Countries”, done with flair.

picks.title: Fromagerie Abraham Kef
picks.blurb: A legendary French cheese shop — sit at the back for a board of raw-milk cheeses and a glass of wine.

picks.title: Café Jakarta
picks.blurb: Indonesian rijsttafel under a soaring, palm-filled atrium at Hotel Jakarta — a jungle dining room out on the IJ.

picks.title: Rijksmuseum
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ treasure house — Rembrandt’s Night Watch, Vermeer and the Golden Age masters in a cathedral of a building.
picks.why: The Night Watch · the big one

picks.title: Stedelijk Museum
picks.blurb: Modern and contemporary art behind the “bathtub” facade — Malevich, De Kooning, Appel and bold rotating shows.

picks.title: Foam
picks.blurb: A nimble photography museum in a canal house — big-name retrospectives and emerging talent, always worth the drop-in.

picks.title: Huis Marseille
picks.blurb: The city’s first photography museum, across two canal houses with a hidden garden — beautifully curated, never crowded.

picks.title: Upstream Gallery
picks.blurb: A contemporary gallery with a digital streak — internet-age artists like Rafaël Rozendaal and Jonas Lund.

picks.title: TonTon Club
picks.blurb: An arcade bar where you pay to play — pinball, classic cabinets and Japanese games, with beer and gyoza on tap.
